term: 'atthi kāyo'ti vā panassa sati paccupaṭṭhitā hoti ... anissito ca viharati, na ca kiñci loke upādiyati
preferred_translation: or remembering is simply present: 'There is body,' just enough for knowing and for remembering. One stays without depending on anything and does not take anything in the world personally
alternative_translations: or remembering is established: 'There is body,' just enough for knowing and for remembering. One remains without depending on anything and does not take anything in the world personally
discouraged_translations: he lives independent and clings to nothing in the world
definition: Body-specific closing anchor of the satipaṭṭhāna refrain, pairing bare presence-for-knowing with non-dependence and non-appropriation.
notes: Use this record for the body-branch closing anchor in MN 10. The line is not a metaphysical claim that body exists in the abstract. It marks a practical anchor: enough presence for knowing and remembering, without leaning on anything and without taking anything personally. The same structure governs the felt-experience, feeling-mind, and dhamma branches with the expected noun substitutions.

example_phrases.pali: 'atthi kāyo'ti vā panassa sati paccupaṭṭhitā hoti ... anissito ca viharati, na ca kiñci loke upādiyati
example_phrases.translation: or remembering is simply present: 'There is body,' just enough for knowing and for remembering. One stays without depending on anything and does not take anything in the world personally
example_phrases.notes: Body-branch closing anchor in the MN 10 refrain.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the body-branch closing anchor in the satipaṭṭhāna refrain.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the body-branch closing anchor unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not turn ‘There is body’ into a metaphysical proposition, and do not flatten the closing into generic non-attachment language that loses the repository's taking-personally distinction.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the satipaṭṭhāna closing anchor from drifting into metaphysical existence-language, generic non-attachment rhetoric, or stale clinging-language.
